commit.message:
feat(GA): PagedKVCache rewrite — dual pools, block-level append, gather, get_block_table/get_seq_lens (13 tests)

- Dual numpy float32 backing stores (_k_np/_v_np) for true in-place slice writes
- k_pool / v_pool properties return cached mx.array, invalidated on mutation
- append() uses block-level numpy slice writes: O(chunk×H×D) not O(T×H) allocations
- Working gather() via numpy concatenate + transpose
- get_block_table() → mx.int32 [B, max_blocks] with -1 padding
- get_seq_lens() → mx.int32 [B]
- block_table_and_seq_lens() backward-compat alias
- seq_lengths formula: (len(blks)-1)*block_size + write_ptr[sid]
- TestPagedKVCacheGA: 13 tests covering all new API surfaces
- Fix: use is_mfa_available() not _MFA_AVAILABLE in skipif decorators

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate SQLite from dashboard — read from CSV instead

SQLite consistently fails on Streamlit Cloud's read-only filesystem
regardless of connection mode.  Solution: pipeline exports a complete
dashboard_noticias.csv (noticias + topics via GROUP_CONCAT), dashboard
reads that CSV with pandas — zero SQLite dependency on Streamlit Cloud.

- analisis/exportar_datos.py: new export function (step 10 of pipeline)
- main.py: call exportar_dashboard_data() as final step
- app/app.py: full rewrite — reads CSV, all aggregations via pandas
  (trends, entities, regions computed from DataFrame, no SQLite queries)
- data/salidas/dashboard_noticias.csv: initial export (2374 rows)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add SDF slice heatmap render mode (cycle with m key)

New render mode "SLICE" shows a 2D cross-section of the SDF:
- Evaluates sceneSDF at a horizontal plane (Y = camera target Y)
- Viridis-like color palette: purple (inside) → teal → yellow (outside)
- Contour lines at regular distance intervals
- Bright white line at d=0 (surface boundary)
- Zoom with +/- controls the view scale
- Pan up/down moves the slice plane

Uses half-block rendering for color fidelity.
Useful for debugging SDF shapes and understanding boolean operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Make Places API daily limit dynamic based on days in month

Instead of a static stored limit, compute it as $200 / $0.032 / daysInMonth.
This gives 201 calls/day in 31-day months and 223/day in 28-day months.

feat: v8.5.0 — PyJWT, structured output y mejoras de prompts CoT

Seguridad:
- Reemplazado python-jose (CVE activo, sin mantenimiento) por PyJWT>=2.9.0
- Eliminado passlib de requirements.txt (no se usaba; bcrypt directo)
- datetime.utcnow() → datetime.now(timezone.utc) en api/auth.py

Agentes — salida estructurada:
- AgenteEspecialista acepta output_schema (Pydantic) opcional
- Usa llm.with_structured_output(schema) cuando se provee schema
- factory.py pasa SalidaJurista/SalidaAuditor/SalidaCronista a cada agente
- parse_json_seguro mantiene como fallback (Ollama/dev local)

Prompts — técnicas de prompt engineering:
- CoT con bloque <razonamiento> en los 3 agentes (ignorado por el parser)
- Few-shot en Jurista: ejemplo externas vs. internas
- Árbol de decisión de 4 pasos en Auditor por cada referencia
- Severidad dinámica ALTA/MEDIA/BAJA (antes hardcodeado a ALTA)

Docs: README y ARQUITECTURA actualizados a v8.5.0

commit.message:
fix: triple-fallback DB connection + sidebar diagnostics panel

utils.py: 3-step fallback chain for read-only filesystems —
  1. Normal r/w connection (local / CI)
  2. immutable=1 URI — no file locking
  3. Copy to /tmp — gives SQLite a writable directory as last resort
Each step is smoke-tested with a real sqlite_master page read.

app.py: add "Diagnostico DB" expander in sidebar (visible even when data
fails) showing sqlite version, file size, dir/file writability, and
whether each connection mode succeeds or fails on the current environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add SHAAM token refresh cron job handler (#61)

* T12 revision: add shaam-token-refresh cron job (pg-boss)

After T12 was merged, the plan was updated to include a token refresh
cron job that depends on T-CRON-01 (pg-boss infrastructure). Now that
T-CRON-01 is merged, this adds the missing piece:

- Add findExpiringCredentials() to ShaamCredentialsRepository
  (queries tokens expiring within a configurable buffer window,
  excludes credentials already marked needsReauth)
- Create shaam-token-refresh handler with factory pattern
  (createShaamTokenRefreshHandler receives logger, processes
  each business independently, marks needsReauth on failure)
- Register cron job in shaam plugin (*/15 * * * *, tz: Asia/Jerusalem)
- Swap jobsPlugin/shaamPlugin registration order in app.ts so
  app.boss is available when shaam plugin registers the cron
- Tests: 4 repository tests for findExpiringCredentials,
  4 handler tests covering no-op, single failure, multi-business,
  and skip-not-expiring scenarios

The actual token refresh HTTP call is deferred to T13 — for now
the handler validates decryption works then marks needsReauth.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PusuKWY7uDyjxAPpigiEYo

* refactor: extract test helpers to reduce duplication in shaam-token-refresh tests

SonarQube flagged 14.1% duplication. Extracted `runHandler()` and
`seedExpiringBusiness()` helpers to eliminate repeated setup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PusuKWY7uDyjxAPpigiEYo

* fix: review findings — markNeedsReauth isolation, env cleanup, boss queue creation

1. Wrap markNeedsReauth in try/catch so one DB failure doesn't abort
   remaining businesses in the cron loop.
2. Add afterEach(vi.unstubAllEnvs) to restore env isolation between tests.
3. Add warning log when pg-boss is unavailable so silent skip is visible.
4. Call createQueue before schedule to fix "Queue not found" in
   boss.integration.test.ts (pg-boss requires explicit queue creation).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PusuKWY7uDyjxAPpigiEYo

* fix: S2004 violation and dynamic imports from reviewer findings

- Lift runHandler, seedExpiringBusiness, and logger to module scope
  in shaam-token-refresh.test.ts (SonarQube S2004: no functions inside
  describe blocks).
- Convert dynamic import() of pdf-service and email-service to static
  imports in invoice-service.ts (no circular dependency justifies them).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PusuKWY7uDyjxAPpigiEYo

* fix: deduplicate seedBusiness across test files (SonarQube 6.2%)

Replace inline seedBusiness() in shaam-token-refresh.test.ts and
shaam-credentials-repository.test.ts with shared createUser() +
createTestBusiness() from tests/utils/businesses.ts.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PusuKWY7uDyjxAPpigiEYo

* fix: flip negated condition in shaam plugin (SonarQube)

Use positive condition `if (app.boss)` with the main logic first,
else-branch for the warning log.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PusuKWY7uDyjxAPpigiEYo

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Slow down crystal temple animations for a more ethereal feel

commit.message:
fix(ci): wipe entire pnpm setup directory to fix store corruption (#587)

The previous cleanup only removed ~/setup-pnpm/node_modules/.bin but
the pnpm content-addressable store at store/v10/files/ had stale
entries causing ENOENT errors during pnpm install on VPS runners.
Wiping ~/setup-pnpm entirely forces a clean reinstall.

commit.message:
Simplify version specs to only * and == (#78)

## Summary

- Remove `^` and `>=` version specifiers from docs and tests, keeping
only `*` (latest) and `==` (exact match)
- Update all doc examples: `^1.0.0` → `*`, `>=1.0.0` → `==1.0.0`
- Remove `^` and `>=` rows from version specifier reference table
- Update `test_delegation.py` to use `==` instead of `^`

Companion to strawpot/strawhub#95 which implements the same
simplification on the registry side.

## Test plan

- [x] `test_delegation.py` updated and passes
- [ ] Verify docs render correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: update algorithm brief #61-62 for multiplicative Shapley MSI

Reflects value function change from additive Σ(qCopula-qInd) to
multiplicative game V(S) = [1-Π(1-qCopula)] - [1-Π(1-qInd)],
sample count 192-960 → 7500, and Quasi-Monte Carlo terminology.

Fix mechanical_heart: inline capsule calls instead of function wrapper

Chisel functions declare all params as float, so passing vec3 args
through a function emits them as floats. Inlined capsule calls
directly to avoid the type mismatch.

Limit address verification to once per 30 days per restaurant

Previously all restaurants were cycled through as fast as the daily
reserve allowed (~10 days with 411 restaurants at 40/day).

commit.message:
Fix: remove VITE_API_URL/VITE_WS_URL from netlify.toml build.environment

netlify.toml [build.environment] values override Netlify dashboard env vars,
so the empty defaults were silently overriding the Railway URLs set in the
dashboard. Removing them lets the dashboard values take effect.

(Carousel) loading separate React copies.

feat: add MonotoneFixedPoint + LinftyAlgebraic, update docs to 15 theorems

Integrate two new proved modules into the main build:
- MonotoneFixedPoint.lean: Knaster-Tarski fixed point between sub/super-fixed
  points (axioms: [propext, Quot.sound] — no Classical.choice)
- LinftyAlgebraic.lean: algebraic core of L∞ bound from Aristotle run 224a0625
  (axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound])

Convention fix: fun h => → fun h ↦ per Mathlib playbook.

Update README (11→15 theorems, 4→5 tiers), VERIFICATION_AUDIT (new §1.9,
synced code snippets), Verify.lean (13→17 #print axioms), debt.md (add
stitching opportunities + deferred items).

commit.message:
feat: TASKS improvements — performance fixes, new categories, UI polish

Performance:
- Limit AI classification to 20 calls max per refresh (keyword fallback first)
- limitedParallel(5) concurrency cap prevents 750 simultaneous HTTP requests
- Keyword monitor: 30s startup delay, 2min interval, remove Nitter fetcher
- RSS: reduce items per source from 25 to 12

Features:
- Finance panel: charts, sector heatmap, AI market analysis
- Map: GDELT conflict layer, area news right-click, improved markers
- Header: notification bell with unread count badge
- Settings: source type filtering, notification permission toggle
- StoryCard: MTS severity label, source type lane, share button
- Notification drawer: science/health, sports, tech/cyber, diplomacy sections
- GDELT topics expanded: 13 topic types including health, sport, science
- Sources: 30+ sources with Telegram OSINT, X accounts, regional feeds
- ACLED proxy: verified conflict event data endpoint

implement-issue and create-issue skills. Rewrite improve-skills to
analyze Claude Code's built-in conversation history at
~/.claude/projects/ instead of custom JSONL logs.

Refs #13
